qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated callback endpoint reads request bodies without size limits, enabling memory-exhaustion DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory fixes missing SUPI/UE ID validation in Nudm_SDM path-parameter handlers under `internal/sbi/api_subscriberdatamanagement.go`, while this finding is a different callback-route bug in `internal/sbi/api_httpcallback.go` where `POST /sdm-subscriptions` reads the full request body with `c.GetRawData()` before validation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's null-byte path-parameter root cause. The advisory patch validates `supi` and `ueId` on Subscriber Data Management handlers before those values reach the UDR client, while the only qualifying finding is an unrelated unauthenticated callback body-allocation DoS on `POST /sdm-subscriptions`.
evidence: The advisory summary and patch `88de9fa74a1b3f3522e53b4cfa2d184712ffa4ee` describe null-byte injection in the Nudm_SDM `supi` path parameter and add `validator.IsValidSupi` / `IsValidGpsi` checks in `internal/sbi/api_subscriberdatamanagement.go` before SDM procedures run.

In the vulnerable SDM flow, `HandleGetAmData` reads `c.Params.ByName("supi")` and passes it to `GetAmDataProcedure`, which places that value into `QueryAmDataRequest.UeId` before calling the generated UDR client.

The qualifying finding instead targets `internal/sbi/api_httpcallback.go` on the separate callback route `POST /sdm-subscriptions`, where `c.GetRawData()` buffers the entire attacker-controlled body on an unauthenticated router group and causes a memory-exhaustion DoS.
